invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Commands like LIST, RETR, and STOR can only be issued after a successful authentication sequence (USER followed by PASS).
A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should precede transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
The QUIT command may be issued at any point to terminate the control connection.
If a PASS command results in a failure (e.g., 530), the client can restart the authentication process by sending another USER command.
RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command.
USER.USER: Must be followed by a PASS command for successful authentication.
PASS.PASS: Must follow a USER command.
TYPE.TYPE_CODE: Common values include 'A' for ASCII and 'I' for binary/Image.
PORT.PORT_SPEC: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port components).

The QUIT command may be issued at any point to terminate the control connection.
If a PASS command results in a failure (e.g., 530), the client can restart the authentication process by sending another USER command.
RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command.

status_changes.rationale: The USER command with 331 response is the standard initiation of the auth sequence.
The PASS command with 230 response confirms successful login.
A 530 error response keeps the state in the authentication phase.
Allows for restarting the login sequence.
PASV (227) is the standard transition to prepare a data channel.
PORT (200) is the active mode setup for a data channel.
While often preceded by PASV/PORT, many servers allow direct LIST if a channel was previously established or uses default. 150 response confirms entry.
Standard transition for downloading a file.
QUIT terminates the session from any active state.
REIN (220) is defined to reset the session.
CWD changes directory but maintains the authenticated session state.
Heuristic trace augmentation suggests self-transition for ACCT.
Transition back to authenticated state after LIST completes. PHrasing is slightly ambiguous as completion is a response, not the command itself.
Transition back to authenticated state after MLSD completes.
Transition back to authenticated state after RETR completes.
Transition back to authenticated state after STOR completes.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP requirement supported by RFC 959 and agent analysis.

status_changes.description: A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should precede transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
status_changes.rationale: Standard behavior for establishing the data channel before transfer.

status_changes.description: If a PASS command results in a failure (e.g., 530), the client can restart the authentication process by sending another USER command.
RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command.
status_changes.rationale: FTP protocol specification (RFC 959) requires this sequence.
